Sentences used:
(1) Birds fly and eat.
(2) John is a baby bird.
(3) John does not fly?
Knowledge used:
If some X is a baby bird then X is a bird. Why: assumed basic knowledge.
Proof steps (by contradiction):
(1) If some X is a baby bird then X is a bird  [background knowledge]
(2) John is a baby bird  [sentence 2]
(3) John is a bird  [from steps 1, 2]
(4) If some X is a bird then X performs the flying event sk6 of X  [sentence 1]
(5) John performs the flying event sk6 of John 1  [from steps 3, 4]
(6) If answer holds and X is a fly event and John performs X then X is not an activity  [sentence 3]
(7) Answer holds  [assumption]
(8) If X is a fly event and John performs X then X is not an activity  [from steps 6, 7]
(9) If sk6 of John 1 is a fly event then sk6 of John 1 is not an activity  [from steps 5, 8]
(10) If some X is a bird then sk6 of X is a fly event  [sentence 1]
(11) sk6 of John 1 is a fly event  [from steps 3, 10]
(12) If some X is a bird then the flying event sk6 of X is an activity  [sentence 1]
(13) The flying event sk6 of John 1 is an activity  [from steps 3, 12]
(14) Contradiction  [from steps 9, 11, 13]
